title: Lasagne

overview.description: Layers of rich homemade Bolognese, creamy béchamel, pasta and Parmesan baked until bubbling and golden. A classic Italian comfort food that's perfect for batch cooking.

method: Prepare one batch of Homemade Bolognese and set aside.
To make the bechamel, melt the butter in a saucepan over a medium heat.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk until completely smooth.
Cook gently, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with salt, pepper and a pinch of nutmeg.
Heat the oven to 180°C fan (200°C conventional).
Spread a thin layer of Bolognese over the base of a large ovenproof dish.
Cover with a layer of lasagne sheets.
Spread over more Bolognese followed by a layer of béchamel.
Repeat the layers until all the ingredients have been used, finishing with béchamel.
Bake for 40–45 minutes until golden brown and bubbling.
Leave to rest for at least 15 minutes before serving. This makes it much easier to portion cleanly.

learning.did-you-know: Lasagne originates from the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy, where it's traditionally made with ragù, béchamel and Parmesan rather than mozzarella.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a classic béchamel, assemble layered dishes and why resting baked dishes improves both texture and serving.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the family's favourite meals and an excellent Saturday project. It freezes exceptionally well and makes several future meals with very little extra work.
chapman-tips: Resting the lasagne before serving is just as important as baking it.
Homemade Bolognese makes a noticeable difference.
Freshly grated Parmesan melts far better than pre-grated cheese.
notes.batch-cooking: Bake two while the oven is on. Freeze whole or in individual portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in the oven or microwave until piping hot. Many people think it tastes even better the following day.
